title: silene nutans subsp. insubrica
intro: Silene nutans subsp. insubrica, also known as Mountain Catchfly, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the Caryophyllaceae family. It is a perennial herb with white or pink flowers, traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is known to have anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce pain, soothe wounds, and protect against oxidative stress. Mountain Catchfly can be consumed as a tea, capsule, or tincture, and is often used in herbal preparations to support respiratory health, alleviate digestive issues, and boost the immune system.
